name: Win
readme:
# Web Server

A lightweight, high-performance web server built from scratch in Python, adopting architectural principles from NGINX. This project demonstrates core web server functionality including HTTP protocol handling, configuration parsing, and request routing.

## Features

- **HTTP Protocol Support**: Full HTTP/1.1 request and response parsing
- NGINX-style Configuration: Configuration file syntax familiar to web server administrators
- Route Matching: Intelligent URI routing with longest prefix matching
- Modular Architecture: Separated concerns for HTTP parsing, configuration management, and request handling
- Custom Exception Handling: Comprehensive error handling for incomplete and invalid HTTP messages

## Project Structure

```
.
├── http_parser.py      # HTTP message parsing and validation
├── config_parser.py    # NGINX-style configuration lexer and parser
├── v1.py              # Main server implementation with socket handling
├── server.conf        # Server configuration file
├── LICENSE            # Project license
└── README.md          # This file
```

### Core Components

**http_parser.py**
- `HTTPMessage`: Data class representing HTTP requests and responses
- `HTTPParser`: Parses raw HTTP data into structured messages
- `IncompleteMessageError`: Exception for incomplete HTTP messages
- `InvalidMessageError`: Exception for malformed HTTP messages

**config_parser.py**
- `SimpleConfigLexer`: Tokenizes NGINX-style configuration files
- `ServerConfig`: Represents parsed server configuration with blocks and directives
- Supports comments, quoted strings, and nested configuration blocks

**v1.py**
- `RouteMatcher`: Matches HTTP requests to appropriate handlers based on port and URI
- `Server`: Main server class managing socket operations and client connections

## Installation

### Requirements

- Python 3.6 or higher
- No external dependencies

### Setup

1. Clone the repository:
```bash
git clone <repository-url>
cd <repository-directory>
```

2. Verify the installation:
```bash
python v1.py
```

## Configuration

The server is configured using `server.conf`, which follows NGINX-style syntax:

```nginx
server {
    listen 8080;
    server_name example.com;
    
    location / {
        proxy_pass http://backend;
    }
}
```

Configuration directives include:
- `listen`: Port number for the server to accept connections
- `server_name`: Server name or domain
- `location`: URI path matching and request handling

## Usage

Start the server:
```bash
python v1.py
```

The server will listen on the configured port (default: 8080) and accept incoming HTTP connections.

## Architecture Highlights

### HTTP Parsing
The HTTP parser handles both request and response parsing with proper separation of status line, headers, and message body.

### Configuration Management
The configuration parser uses a lexer-based approach to tokenize NGINX-style configuration files, supporting nested blocks and various directive types.

### Request Routing
Routes are matched using longest URI prefix matching, allowing fine-grained control over request handling for different paths.

## Development

To extend this server:

1. Add new directives in `config_parser.py`
2. Implement handler logic in `v1.py`
3. Test with sample HTTP requests

## License

This project is licensed under the LICENSE file included in the repository. See [LICENSE](LICENSE) for details.

## Contributing

Contributions are welcome. Please ensure code follows the existing style and includes appropriate documentation.

## Future Development

This project serves as a foundation for learning web server architecture. Development will continue with further enhancements and will be working on further developing much bigger ones.

name: smartpocket
description: SmartPocket is a web-based application that empowers teenagers to manage their pocket money using natural voice commands. It blends the power of voice AI with financial literacy, gamification, and a teen-friendly design to build smart money habits from an early age.
readme:
# 🧠 SmartPocket – Voice-Based Money Management for Teenagers

SmartPocket is a web-based application that empowers teenagers to manage their pocket money using natural voice commands. It blends the power of voice AI with financial literacy, gamification, and a teen-friendly design to build smart money habits from an early age.

## 💡 Problem Statement

Teenagers often struggle to manage their pocket money due to a lack of simple and engaging financial tools. Existing apps are either too complex or not designed for young users. There's a growing need for a platform that helps teens track expenses, build saving habits, and learn about money — in a way that's intuitive and fun.

---

## 🎯 Project Goal

To build an interactive, voice-enabled platform that allows teenagers to:
- Log income and expenses using natural language
- Track savings goals
- View spending trends
- Learn financial concepts through daily quizzes
- Get personalized saving tips

TO RUN FRONTEND:
cd project
npm run dev

TO RUN BACKEND:
cd project
cd server
node index.js

UI image:
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/34e7253c-edbd-4eb2-a554-5a2c81cf32ef)

name: vocaleyes
readme:
Hello! This is VocalEyes Vocal Eyes is a minimalist accessibility web app designed to help blind and visually impaired users understand their surroundings. It allows users to trigger the app with a voice command (“Scan”), capture a photo automatically, generate a description of the scene using AI, and then listen to the description via text-to-speech — all in a seamless flow. Features 🧠 AI-Powered Description – Generates a short description of the scene.
🔊 Text-to-Speech – Reads the description out loud.
🔒 Minimal UI – Designed for accessibility and ease of use.
🌍 Multi-Language Support – Works in 10+ languages, configurable by the user.
🤖 Google Assistant Integration – Open the app by saying “Scan”.
🚀 Getting Started 1. Clone the repository ```Bash git clone https://github.com/Daiwik2005/vocal-eyes.git cd vocal-eyes ``` 2. Install dependencies ```Bash pip install -r requirements.txt ``` 3. Add your API keys (Important) Streamlit requires secrets to be stored in a .streamlit/secrets.toml file. Create a folder named .streamlit in your project root and add a secrets.toml file like this:

# .streamlit/secrets.toml


```Bash [api_keys] gemini_api = "your_gemini_api_key_here" # Add other keys here if needed ``` 🔑 Without this file, the app will not work. 4. Run the app ```Bash streamlit run app.py ``` 🛠️ Tech Stack Frontend: Streamlit (minimalist web UI)
AI Model: Google Gemini API (for image description)
TTS: Streamlit / Python text-to-speech libraries
Voice Assistant: Google Assistant Action to launch app
Enjoy!!

name: mindful-spend-ai
readme:
This is **Reflectify.ai**
_Helping your mind and wallet make smarter choices — together._ **What Is Reflectify AI?** Have you ever stared at your cart and thought: “Do I really need this… or am I just emotionally unstable right now?” 😅 **Reflectify AI is your mindful decision coach.**
It uses AI + behavioral psychology to help you slow down, reflect, and make better purchase (and life) choices — before you click Buy Now. We’re not another “budgeting” app — we’re building an AI companion that improves decision awareness, targeting the intersection of fintech + mental health. Our data shows: -85% of users reconsider a purchase after a 24-hour delay.
-Emotional recognition = higher retention and trust.
-The market for digital mindfulness + consumer control is booming. _Think of it as a mirror for your decisions, powered by empathy and algorithms._ **Why It Exists?** We live in a world of dopamine-driven decisions. Scroll → Click → Regret → Repeat.
Reflectify AI is here to break that cycle — gently. It doesn’t judge, it just helps you pause and ask, “Is this really what I want… or just what I feel like right now?” **How It Works?(The Science of Reflection™)** -Decision Quiz Before buying something, answer quick, mindful prompts: “Is this a need or a want?” “Will you still want this next week?” “Are you buying to solve a problem or fill a feeling?” -Reflection Timeline
Adds a gentle delay before impulsive purchases.
You can “lock” an item for 1-12 hours and revisit it later.
Most people never come back 😅 -AI Insight Engine
Reflectify learns from your past patterns — not to judge, but to understand your emotional spending habits. It’s like journaling meets machine learning. -Emotion-Aware Feedback
The app subtly detects mood triggers (like boredom or stress) and responds with friendly nudges or affirmations. -Mindful Notification System
Reflectify doesn’t just sit quietly in your app drawer — it shows up when you need it most. Sends gentle reminders when you’re about to make an impulsive buy. Nudges you with “reflective moments” throughout the day: “Hey night owl 🌙, are you shopping to relax or escape stress?” Celebrates reflection streaks with positive notes — because reflection deserves rewards too. 🥳 Notifications are timed based on your habits — not random spam. **Tech Stack** | Layer | Tech | | ------------ | ---------------------------------------------------------------------- | | 🖥️ Frontend | Vite + React 19 + TypeScript + Tailwind v4 + ShadCN UI + Framer Motion | | 🔙 Backend | Convex (for serverless functions, database, auth) | | 🧠 AI Core | For now we have used Gemini APi directly,but will be making a model | | 🔐 Auth | Convex Auth (Email OTP + Anonymous mode) | | 💾 Data | Convex database (user patterns, quiz insights, reflection logs) | | 🎨 Design | Minimal, emotionally balanced interface (calm colors + smooth motion) | **To Run locally:** ``` pnpm install npx convex dev pnpm run dev ``` **Environment** ``` CONVEX_DEPLOYMENT=anonymous:anonymous-reflectify-ai VITE_CONVEX_URL=http://127.0.0.1:3210 SITE_URL=http://localhost:5173 ```

# 🧠 SmartPocket – Voice-Based Money Management for Teenagers

SmartPocket is a web-based application that empowers teenagers to manage their pocket money using natural voice commands. It blends the power of voice AI with financial literacy, gamification, and a teen-friendly design to build smart money habits from an early age.

## 💡 Problem Statement

Teenagers often struggle to manage their pocket money due to a lack of simple and engaging financial tools. Existing apps are either too complex or not designed for young users. There's a growing need for a platform that helps teens track expenses, build saving habits, and learn about money — in a way that's intuitive and fun.

---

## 🎯 Project Goal

To build an interactive, voice-enabled platform that allows teenagers to:
- Log income and expenses using natural language
- Track savings goals
- View spending trends
- Learn financial concepts through daily quizzes

name: document-verification
readme:
Document Verification Project (Hackathon)

This is my first hackathon project, and I'm thrilled to share it with you! The Document Verification Dashboard is designed to automate the document verification process, making it much more efficient than the usual manual methods, whether dealing with digital or physical documents. 📑✨

The system leverages OCR (Optical Character Recognition) 🤖 and other automated technologies to instantly identify and validate documents directly through an API 🔑. This means that companies or organizations can quickly verify documents sourced from government databases without the need for manual intervention, reducing human error and speeding up the entire process. ⚡

The dashboard is user-friendly, enabling easy access to document verification tasks. By automating what is traditionally a time-consuming and error-prone process, we aim to make the verification process faster, more accurate, and more scalable. 

Note: This project was developed in response to the Smart India Hackathon (SIH) DRDO problem statement, addressing a real-world requirement for faster document validation.

name: parking-mate
readme:
# 🚗 Vehicle Parking Management System (V1)

The **Vehicle Parking Management System** is a web-based application that helps manage parking lots efficiently.  
It provides separate roles for **Admins** and **Users**, ensuring smooth allocation and monitoring of parking spaces.  

---

## ✨ Features

### 👨‍💼 Admin
- Create and manage **parking lots**  
- Add or remove **parking spots**  
- View and monitor overall **parking availability**  

### 🙋 User
- Reserve a parking spot  
- Release a spot once done  
- View **real-time availability** of spots  

---

## 🗄️ Database
- Uses **SQLite** for lightweight and local storage  
- Database is **programmatically created** on setup (no need for manual creation)  

---

## 💻 Tech Stack
- **Backend**: Flask (Python)  
- **Frontend**: HTML, CSS, Bootstrap (via Jinja2 templating)  
- **Database**: SQLite  

---

## 🚀 Getting Started

### 1. Clone the repository
```bash
git clone https://github.com/yourusername/parking-management.git
cd parking-management
```

### 2. Create and activate a virtual environment (optional but recommended)
```bash
python -m venv venv
source venv/bin/activate   # On macOS/Linux
venv\Scripts\activate      # On Windows
```

### 3. Install dependencies
```bash
pip install -r requirements.txt
```

### 4. Run the application
```bash
python app.py
```

### The app will run locally at:
```bash
http://127.0.0.1:5000/
```

name: Basic-Image-Caption-Model
readme:
📸 Image Caption Generator

This project is a simple AI-powered web app that generates captions for images using the BLIP model from Hugging Face and Streamlit for the interface.

Features
-->Upload an image from your computer
-->Get an AI-generated description (caption) for the image
-->Powered by Salesforce BLIP
-->Runs locally — your images and captions stay private



Installation

Clone this repository or download the files

```
git clone https://github.com/yourusername/image-caption-generator.git
cd image-caption-generator
```


Create and activate a virtual environment (recommended)

```
python -m venv venv
venv\Scripts\activate   # On Windows
source venv/bin/activate  # On Mac/Linux
```


Install dependencies

```
pip install -r requirements.txt
```

▶️ Usage

Run the Streamlit app:

```
streamlit run app.py
```


A local URL will be shown (e.g., http://localhost:8501)

Open it in your browser

Upload an image and get a caption!
Enjoy
